epic_name: AI Co-Pilot Intelligence — Memory, Skills & Dynamic Context
notes: Frontend stories covered: E5b-5 (Memory & Skills Management UI) and E5b-7 (Inline Entity Mentions & Chat Autocomplete). Stories E5b-1 through E5b-4 and E5b-6 are backend-only and tested via the backend test plan.

prerequisites.auth: Authenticated user session with ADMIN role (for skill override tests) and STAFF role (for read-only verification)
prerequisites.seed_data: AI memory seeded with 10+ memories across 5 categories (PREFERENCE, WORKFLOW, DECISION, INSTRUCTION, ENTITY_CONTEXT), mix of EXPLICIT and IMPLICIT sources. Skills seeded via E5b-6 views skill pack. Entity triggers seeded for 'view' and 'saved view'.
prerequisites.backend: API server running with AI module registered, memory endpoints active, skills endpoints active, entity-triggers endpoint active, entity-search proxy active

journeys.name: AI Section Appears in Sidebar Navigation
journeys.description: Verify the AI sidebar section exists with all 3 navigation items: Morning Briefing, My Memory, Skills

steps.expected_result: App shell loads with sidebar visible
steps.visual_check: App shell with purple sidebar, top bar with 'N' logo, sidebar shows module sections

steps.target: Sidebar AI section with Bot icon
steps.expected_result: AI section is visible in the sidebar with Bot icon and 'AI' label

steps.target: AI section expand toggle in sidebar
steps.expected_result: AI section expands to show 3 navigation items
steps.visual_check: Expanded AI section showing: 'Morning Briefing' (Sun icon), 'My Memory' (Brain icon), 'Skills' (Wand2 icon)

steps.expected_result: All 3 items visible: 'Morning Briefing', 'My Memory', 'Skills'

journeys.name: Memory Management Page Load & Layout
journeys.description: Navigate to the Memory page and verify the T7 settings template layout loads correctly with settings panel, stats, and memory list

steps.target: My Memory link in AI sidebar section
steps.expected_result: Navigates to /ai/memory
Page title shows 'My Memory' with Brain icon
steps.visual_check: Memory page with Concept D styling: light purple (#f4f2ff) background, purple accents, settings panel at top, memory list below. Skeleton loading may be visible briefly.

steps.expected_result: Settings panel shows: 'Enable AI Memory' toggle (on), category checkboxes (all checked), retention period selector, 'Forget Everything' button
Stats panel shows total memory count, Explicit vs Learned breakdown with percentages

steps.target: Search input and category filter pills
steps.expected_result: Search input with placeholder 'Search memories...' and category filter pills visible
Memories displayed grouped by category (Preferences, Workflows, Decisions, Instructions, Entity Context) with collapsible accordion sections and count badges
steps.visual_check: Memory cards with 12px radius, category badges (coloured), source badges (Explicit=purple, Learned=grey), creation dates, last used dates. Cards appear with fadeInUp animation.

journeys.name: Toggle Memory Settings
journeys.description: Test toggling memory enable/disable and category checkboxes with optimistic updates

steps.expected_result: Memory page loads with settings panel
Toggle switches to OFF, category checkboxes and retention selector become visually disabled
steps.visual_check: Toggle is OFF (grey), category checkboxes are greyed out/disabled, retention selector is disabled. Memory list remains visible but settings are inactive.

steps.expected_result: Toggle switches back to ON, category checkboxes and retention selector become active again
Workflows category is unchecked, PATCH /ai/memories/settings called with updated enabledCategories
Workflows category is re-checked
Retention period changes to 90 days
steps.visual_check: Retention period selector shows '90 days' selected

journeys.name: Search and Filter Memories
journeys.description: Test client-side search and category filtering of the memory list

steps.expected_result: Memory page loads with all memories visible
After 300ms debounce, memory list filters to show only memories containing 'invoice' (case-insensitive)
steps.visual_check: Only memory cards containing the word 'invoice' are visible, other categories/cards hidden

steps.expected_result: List further filters to show only Preferences that also contain 'invoice'
Search text cleared, showing all Preferences (category filter still active)
Empty state shown: 'No memories match your search'
steps.visual_check: Empty state message visible with 'No memories match your search' text

journeys.name: Edit an Existing Memory
journeys.description: Test editing a memory's content via the edit dialog with optimistic update

steps.expected_result: Memory page loads with memories visible

steps.target: Edit button (pencil icon) on first memory card
steps.expected_result: Edit dialog opens with 'Edit Memory' title and memory content in a textarea
steps.visual_check: Dialog overlay with 'Edit Memory' heading, textarea pre-filled with current memory content, Save and Cancel buttons

steps.target: Memory content textarea in edit dialog

input_data.content: Updated preference: Always use Net 60 payment terms

steps.expected_result: Textarea shows new content, Save button is enabled
Dialog closes, memory card updates immediately (optimistic), toast notification shows 'Memory updated'
steps.visual_check: Memory card shows updated content 'Updated preference: Always use Net 60 payment terms', success toast visible at top-right

journeys.name: Delete a Single Memory
journeys.description: Test deleting a single memory with confirmation dialog and fade-out animation

steps.expected_result: Memory page loads

steps.target: Memory category group with count badge
steps.expected_result: Note the count badge number for the target category

steps.target: Delete button (trash icon) on a memory card
steps.expected_result: Confirmation dialog appears with 'Delete this memory? This action cannot be undone.'
steps.visual_check: AlertDialog with warning message, memory content excerpt visible, red destructive 'Delete' button and 'Cancel' button

steps.expected_result: Dialog closes, memory card removed with animation, toast shows 'Memory deleted', category count badge decrements
steps.visual_check: Memory card gone from list, count badge updated, success toast 'Memory deleted' visible

journeys.name: Forget Everything — Destructive Action
journeys.description: Test the 'Forget Everything' flow requiring user to type 'FORGET' to confirm

steps.expected_result: Memory page loads with memories

steps.target: Forget Everything button (red variant) in settings panel
steps.expected_result: Destructive confirmation dialog appears with warning icon, title 'Forget Everything'
steps.visual_check: AlertDialog with warning icon, title 'Forget Everything', body text 'This will permanently delete ALL of your AI memories. This action cannot be undone.', text input for confirmation, confirm button disabled

steps.expected_result: Confirm button remains disabled (incomplete word)
Confirm button becomes enabled (red variant)
steps.visual_check: Text input shows 'FORGET', red 'Forget Everything' confirm button is now enabled/clickable

steps.expected_result: Dialog closes, memory list clears with animation, toast shows 'All memories have been deleted', empty state displayed
steps.visual_check: Memory list is empty, empty state message 'No memories yet' with Lightbulb icon visible, success toast 'All memories have been deleted'

journeys.name: Memory Page Empty State
journeys.description: Verify the empty state renders correctly when no memories exist

steps.expected_result: Memory page loads (after forget-all or with empty memory store)
Empty state shows Lightbulb icon, 'No memories yet' heading, 'As you interact with the AI, it will remember your preferences and decisions' description
steps.visual_check: Centered empty state with Lightbulb icon, motivational message, Concept D purple styling, no memory cards visible

steps.expected_result: Stats panel shows Total: 0, Explicit: 0, Learned: 0

journeys.name: AI Skills Browser Page Load & Layout
journeys.description: Navigate to the Skills page and verify the module-grouped accordion layout

steps.target: Skills link in AI sidebar section
steps.expected_result: Navigates to /ai/skills
Page title shows 'AI Skills' with Wand2 icon
steps.visual_check: Skills page with Concept D styling: light purple background, search input, module filter dropdown, accordion sections grouped by module

steps.target: First module accordion group (expanded by default)
steps.expected_result: First module group (e.g., 'Views & Navigation') is expanded showing skill cards with skill count badge
Each skill card shows: name, description, trigger phrases as green tags, orchestration pattern badge, active/inactive status indicator (green dot)
steps.visual_check: Skill cards with 12px radius, trigger phrases as green pill tags, negative triggers as red tags (if any), green dot 'Active' indicator, orchestration pattern badge

journeys.name: Search and Filter Skills
journeys.description: Test client-side search and module filtering on the Skills page

steps.expected_result: Skills page loads with all skills visible
After 300ms debounce, skills list filters to show only skills whose name, description, or trigger phrases match 'invoice'
steps.visual_check: Only skills matching 'invoice' in name, description, or trigger phrases are visible

steps.expected_result: Skills list filters to show only skills in the 'Views & Navigation' module
Empty state shown: 'No skills match your search'
steps.visual_check: Empty state with Zap icon and 'No skills match your search' message

journeys.name: Admin Skill Detail & Override (ADMIN Role)
journeys.description: Test that an ADMIN user can view skill details and save/reset overrides

steps.expected_result: Skills page loads (logged in as ADMIN)
Slide-out sheet opens from right showing full skill details
steps.visual_check: Sheet with skill name, active/inactive toggle, editable trigger phrases as tag input, editable priority number, read-only skill instructions in monospace, read-only required tools list, Save Override and Reset to Default buttons at bottom

steps.target: Active/Inactive toggle in skill detail sheet
steps.expected_result: Toggle switches state (active → inactive or vice versa)
New trigger phrase 'display records' added as a green tag
Priority value changes to 150
Override saved via PUT /ai/skill-overrides/:skillId, toast shows 'Skill override saved', sheet closes, skill card updates to reflect override
steps.visual_check: Toast 'Skill override saved' visible, skill card in list may show 'Custom' override badge

steps.expected_result: Sheet re-opens showing the overridden values
Override removed via DELETE /ai/skill-overrides/:skillId, toast shows 'Override removed — using default settings', skill card reverts to default values
steps.visual_check: Toast 'Override removed — using default settings' visible, skill card no longer shows override badge

journeys.name: Staff Skill Detail — Read-Only (STAFF Role)
journeys.description: Verify that STAFF users see read-only skill details with no edit controls

steps.expected_result: Skills page loads (logged in as STAFF user)
Slide-out sheet opens with all fields read-only
steps.visual_check: Sheet shows skill details but NO save button, NO toggles, NO editable inputs. Only a Close button at the bottom. Trigger phrases displayed as non-editable tags.

steps.expected_result: Only Close button visible, no Save Override or Reset to Default buttons

journeys.name: Concept D Visual Compliance Check
journeys.description: Verify both Memory and Skills pages follow Concept D design system requirements

steps.expected_result: Memory page loads
Page rendered with Concept D styling
steps.visual_check: Purple primary (#7c3aed) on action buttons and toggles, cards with 12px border-radius and custom shadow, light purple (#f4f2ff) background, Plus Jakarta Sans headings, Inter body text, focus rings on interactive elements, skeleton loading completed

steps.expected_result: Skills page loads
Page rendered with Concept D styling
steps.visual_check: Category badges with semantic colours, green trigger phrase tags, red negative trigger tags, purple active states, cards with purple-tinted hover shadow, fadeInUp animation visible on card entry

steps.target: Keyboard focus rings on interactive elements
steps.expected_result: Tab through interactive elements — each shows a visible focus ring (WCAG 2.1 AA compliance)

journeys.name: Memory Page Responsive Behaviour
journeys.description: Test memory page layout at phone, tablet, and desktop breakpoints

steps.expected_result: Phone layout: single column, settings sections stack vertically, accordion for categories
steps.visual_check: Phone viewport (375px): single column layout, settings panel stacked vertically, category sections as accordion, memory cards full-width, touch-friendly button sizes

steps.expected_result: Tablet layout: single column form layout
steps.visual_check: Tablet viewport (768px): single column but wider form layout, settings panel and memory list full-width

steps.expected_result: Desktop layout: two-column settings panel (label left, input right), full memory/skill cards
steps.visual_check: Desktop viewport (1440px): two-column settings layout, wider memory cards, more content visible

journeys.name: Entity Mention Trigger Word Detection in Chat
journeys.description: Test that typing trigger words in the copilot chat input activates entity autocomplete

steps.expected_result: Dashboard loads
Co-Pilot drawer opens
steps.visual_check: Co-Pilot drawer visible on right side (desktop) with chat history, message area, quick prompts, and input area with EntityMentionInput

steps.expected_result: After typing 'saved view' trigger word + 2+ chars 'ov', autocomplete dropdown appears above the input showing matching saved views
steps.visual_check: Autocomplete dropdown positioned above the input, showing matching saved view entities with: purple circle icon (Bookmark), display name, subtitle, purple highlight on selected item. Dropdown has 12px border-radius matching Concept D.

steps.expected_result: Footer text visible: 'Use arrow keys to navigate, Enter to select, Esc to dismiss'
No autocomplete dropdown appears (no trigger word detected)

journeys.name: Entity Selection & Chip Insertion
journeys.description: Test selecting an entity from autocomplete and verifying chip insertion in the input

steps.expected_result: Co-Pilot drawer opens
Autocomplete dropdown appears showing matching views (e.g., 'Users' DataView)
Trigger word + search text replaced with entity chip. Chip shows entity name in purple pill style.
steps.visual_check: Entity chip visible in input area: purple pill (bg-[#ede9fe] text-[#6d28d9]) with LayoutList icon, 'Users' text, and X close button. Autocomplete dropdown dismissed. Input textarea refocused.

steps.expected_result: Additional text typed after the chip, input shows chip + text together

steps.target: X close button on entity chip
steps.expected_result: Entity chip removed from input, text remains

journeys.name: Send Message with Entity Mentions & Display in Chat
journeys.description: Test sending a message with entity mentions and verifying they render as chips in the chat history

steps.expected_result: Co-Pilot drawer opens
Autocomplete appears
Entity chip inserted
Text added after chip

steps.target: Send button (purple arrow) in chat input
steps.expected_result: Message sent with entity mentions. User message bubble appears in chat with entity chips displayed inline.
steps.visual_check: User message bubble (purple/primary background) with entity chip using user-message variant (bg-white/20 text-white), followed by message text 'show me all records'. Input cleared and ready for next message.

steps.expected_result: AI assistant streaming indicator (pulsing dots) visible while waiting for response
Assistant message appears with response. If it references entities, they render as assistant-message variant chips (bg-[#ede9fe] text-[#6d28d9]).
steps.visual_check: Assistant message bubble (light background) with any entity mentions as muted purple chips, non-removable (display-only). Message content addresses the user's request about the mentioned entity.

journeys.name: Entity Autocomplete Keyboard Navigation
journeys.description: Test full keyboard navigation of entity autocomplete: arrow keys, Enter, Tab, Escape, Backspace

steps.expected_result: Co-Pilot drawer opens
Autocomplete dropdown appears with results
Second result highlighted with purple background (#f5f3ff)
steps.visual_check: Autocomplete dropdown with second item highlighted in light purple (#f5f3ff), first item unhighlighted

steps.expected_result: First result highlighted again
Selected entity inserted as chip, autocomplete closes
Autocomplete appears again for 'saved view' trigger
Autocomplete dropdown dismissed, text remains in input
Textarea is empty, chip(s) still present from step 5

steps.target: Press Backspace key with empty textarea
steps.expected_result: Last entity chip removed from input
steps.visual_check: Input area with no chips remaining, empty textarea, ready for fresh input
